scenario: Node.js version mismatch: environment check fails
expected_behavior: Skill must verify runtime matches RULES.md (or project constraints). On mismatch, report: 'Node.js version mismatch: required 18.17.0 but have 16.19.0'. BLOCK setup until correct version is installed (nvm install 18.17.0 or use runtime manager). Do NOT proceed to npm ci until runtime matches.
notes: Environment validation must happen BEFORE branch checkout. Version mismatch is BLOCKED state.

expected_behavior: Skill must detect Docker dependency and verify Docker daemon is running. Report: 'Docker required for this task. Starting docker-compose up -d'. Wait for containers to be healthy. Confirm: 'docker ps' shows all required services. Only then proceed to workspace verification.
notes: Docker setup is prerequisite for workspace validation. Check service health before declaring environment READY.

scenario: Workspace pollution: uncommitted changes from prior task
expected_behavior: Skill must run 'git status' and confirm workspace is clean. If uncommitted changes exist, report: 'Workspace has uncommitted changes from prior task — contamination risk.' Block setup and instruct: 'Either commit or git stash these changes before proceeding.' Verify with 'git status' again before declaring workspace clean.
notes: Leftover state from previous task invalidates environment. Must explicitly check 'git status' output for untracked files and modified files.

expected_behavior: Skill must run 'npm ci' (not 'npm install') to install dependencies pinned in lock file. Report: 'Installing dependencies from package-lock.json (pinned versions)'. Verify completion with 'npm ls' to show dependency tree. Confirm all dev dependencies are present. Only then declare environment installation DONE.
notes: Use 'npm ci' for lock file consistency, not 'npm install' which can upgrade versions.

scenario: Task assignment: next task identification from TASK_DAG.md
expected_behavior: Skill must read TASK_DAG.md and identify first task where all dependencies are [DONE]. If TASK-5 is blocked, select TASK-4 instead. Confirm with user: 'Next task is TASK-4: <title>. Starting this now — confirm?' Only after user confirmation proceed to branch setup.
notes: Task selection must respect DAG dependencies. Never skip dependency verification.

scenario: Branch setup: worktree vs standard mode
expected_behavior: Skill must offer choice: standard mode (single branch) or worktree mode (parallel branches). For parallel development, create: 'git worktree add ../task-9-<slug> -b task-9-<slug>'. Change to new worktree directory. Verify separate .git/worktrees/<slug> exists. Each worktree operates independently without stash switching.
notes: Worktree mode enables parallel development on DAG tasks. Standard mode for sequential workflow.
